scenarios.journey: A consumer parses widgets.json at the repo root with a strict JSON parser (python3 json.load — no leniency for trailing commas or garbage) and gets an array whose every entry is a string and which equals the non-empty lines of widget.txt element-for-element, in order. Independent comparison, not the repo's own check script. Result: ["the widget"].

A new contributor opens README.md to learn what widgets.json is, and finds a section stating what it contains (a JSON array of widget name strings), that it derives from the non-empty lines of widget.txt in order, and the regeneration story (manual, no generator script).

The branch-recorded gate resolves feat/e2e-1-machine-readable-widget-list on github.com/barry-napier/tracker-gh-proof and the remote ref SHA equals the local frozen tip b7ac1b9 — verified via git ls-remote against github.com directly, bypassing the local-path 'origin' remote that caused the run-1 bounce.

The pr-fresh gate finds an OPEN PR (#2) for the branch into main whose headRefOid equals the branch tip b7ac1b9 — verified via the GitHub API (gh pr view), past the endpoint: state, base branch, and head SHA all asserted, not just PR existence.
The ticket's own acceptance suite (checks/ac-1.sh through ac-4.sh, per checks/manifest.json) runs end to end at the frozen SHA and every script prints PASS and exits 0.
